summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of UGS HireFlow MVP. Backend: 34/34 pytest tests pass covering auth, RBAC, candidates full lifecycle (create/verify/assign/interview/payment/partner/notes), employees, companies, jobs (with candidate allocation), batches, notifications, activity logs, dashboards (admin/employee/candidate), global search, reports (placements/summary), CSV export/import, and public registration. Fixed one 500 bug in POST /api/employees (ObjectId serialization). Frontend: login flow works, admin dashboard renders all 8 KPIs (Total Candidates, Pending Verification, Placed, In Pipeline, Employees, Companies, Open Jobs, Active Batches), sidebar navigation works across all 9 sections, candidates list + detail with all 6 tabs (Overview/Pipeline/Documents/Payments/Partner/Activity) renders, theme toggle works, notifications popover works, global search returns results, public register page loads.

minor.issue: Returned 500 due to unpopped MongoDB _id (ObjectId not JSON serializable). FIXED in this iteration by adding user_doc.pop('_id', None).

CandidateCreate model (extends CandidateBase) does not expose registration_fee; admins must PATCH the candidate afterwards to set fee. Consider adding registration_fee to CandidateBase for a smoother create flow.

No role-based restriction on listing — a logged-in candidate can list all candidates (list endpoint restricts only for role=employee). Detail endpoint does restrict candidate role. Consider adding candidate scoping on the list too.
PaymentCreate uses field 'payment_mode' but frontend/tests commonly send 'mode'. Not a bug per se — extra fields are ignored — worth documenting.

ui_bugs.issue: Recharts logs repeated warnings 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' during initial mount — chart container has no explicit min-height/width causing recharts to complain. Cosmetic but should be fixed by wrapping ResponsiveContainer with a fixed-height parent.

action_items: Optional: expose registration_fee in CandidateCreate model (models.py) so admins can set fee at creation.
Optional: apply candidate-role scoping on GET /api/candidates list endpoint.
Fix Recharts container sizing warnings on Admin Dashboard (wrap chart in a div with explicit height like h-64).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:394 fixed: was returning user_doc without stripping MongoDB _id → 500. Same pattern exists in other endpoints but they use find_one(..., {'_id':0}) or Pydantic model_dump(); double-check similar handlers if new endpoints are added.
server.py is 788 lines and is getting large — recommend splitting into routers (auth, candidates, employees, jobs, dashboards) for maintainability.
server.py:400 update_employee accepts raw dict body which strips password/role but does not Pydantic-validate the payload — consider tightening.
Recharts container in Admin Dashboard emits width/height warnings — wrap in fixed-height parent.
seed_data_creation: Seed data auto-loaded by startup hook (1 admin, 2 employees, 10 candidates, 5 companies, 10 jobs, 2 batches). Test suite creates its own TEST_-prefixed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints fully covered by /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with `pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Sidebar test-ids use lowercased label with dashes: sidebar-dashboard, sidebar-candidates, sidebar-pipeline, sidebar-companies, sidebar-job-openings, sidebar-batches, sidebar-employees, sidebar-reports, sidebar-activity-log, sidebar-settings. Notification button is data-testid='notifications-btn', theme is 'theme-toggle', search is 'global-search-input', login form uses login-email/login-password/login-submit.
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/employees 500: motor's insert_one mutates the passed dict in-place to add _id (ObjectId), and the handler returned that dict via FastAPI which failed JSON encoding. Fix: pop('_id', None) before returning. Root pattern to watch across all POST handlers that return the inserted dict directly.
